cards.front: Because I'm very tired, I'm irritable.
cards.back: 因为很累，所以我很烦躁。
cards.romanized: Yīnwèi hěn lèi, suǒyǐ wǒ hěn fánzào.
cards.grammarNote: In Chinese, 因为 and 所以 are usually used together: because...therefore... This differs from English, where you pick one (because OR so, not both).

cards.front: Because he was late, everyone was angry.
cards.back: 因为他迟到了，所以大家都很生气。
cards.romanized: Yīnwèi tā chídào le, suǒyǐ dàjiā dōu hěn shēngqì.

cards.front: Because the weather is nice, I'm happy.
cards.back: 因为天气很好，所以我很开心。
cards.romanized: Yīnwèi tiānqì hěn hǎo, suǒyǐ wǒ hěn kāixīn.

cards.front: Because there's too much work, I'm anxious.
cards.back: 因为工作太多，所以我很焦虑。
cards.romanized: Yīnwèi gōngzuò tài duō, suǒyǐ wǒ hěn jiāolǜ.

cards.front: Because it rained, we cancelled our plans.
cards.back: 因为下雨，我们取消了计划。
cards.romanized: Yīnwèi xià yǔ, wǒmen qǔxiāo le jìhuà.
cards.grammarNote: 因为 can appear alone (without 所以) when giving a reason, especially answering a 为什么 question.

cards.front: Because she was very sad, she cried.
cards.romanized: Yīnwèi tā hěn nánguò, tā kū le.

cards.front: He was late, so I was angry.
cards.back: 他迟到了，所以我很生气。
cards.romanized: Tā chídào le, suǒyǐ wǒ hěn shēngqì.
cards.grammarNote: 所以 can appear alone when the cause is already known or has been stated. It introduces the result/consequence.

cards.front: I didn't sleep well, so I'm very tired today.
cards.back: 我没睡好，所以今天很累。
cards.romanized: Wǒ méi shuì hǎo, suǒyǐ jīntiān hěn lèi.

cards.front: She was very upset, so she cried.
cards.romanized: Tā hěn nánguò, suǒyǐ kū le.

cards.front: Because the news was good, everyone was excited.
cards.back: 因为消息很好，所以大家都很兴奋。
cards.romanized: Yīnwèi xiāoxi hěn hǎo, suǒyǐ dàjiā dōu hěn xīngfèn.

cards.front: Because I haven't heard from him, I'm worried.
cards.back: 因为没有他的消息，所以我很担心。
cards.romanized: Yīnwèi méiyǒu tā de xiāoxi, suǒyǐ wǒ hěn dānxīn.

cards.front: Because it went well, I feel relieved.
cards.back: 因为进行顺利，所以我感到宽慰。
cards.romanized: Yīnwèi jìnxíng shùnlì, suǒyǐ wǒ gǎndào kuānwèi.
